import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate.StalkTensor
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate.Vestigial
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate.PresheafInternalHom
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.LineBundlePullback

/-!
# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)

This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
`Pic^РЎ»_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ├Ќ_k T) / ¤ђ_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).

The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
`[L] + [L'] := [L РіЌ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
ingredients on the Lean carrier:

1. a binary tensor-product operation
   `РіЌ : Scheme.Modules X ├Ќ Scheme.Modules X Рєњ Scheme.Modules X`;
2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `РіЌ`;
3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
   the dual `LРЂ╗┬╣ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.

At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.

## Status (current)

`tensorObj` and `tensorObj_functoriality` are fully defined (no `sorry`), lifting
`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through sheafification on the small Zariski
site. Tracked typed-`sorry` residuals (current):
1. (deferred) `РіЌ`-inverse lane (`exists_tensorObj_inverse`, MOVED iter-023 to
   `TensorObjInverse.lean` Рђћ closes via the dual chain in `DualInverse.lean`).
2. (seed-1, active) `lhs_tmul`: the SOLE in-file sorry is
   `pushforward_lax_mu_comparison_lhs_tmul` Рђћ the LHS pure-tensor mate value (the
   ┬х-side sectionwise residual of K1's `hcompat`).  `hmon : hadj'.IsMonoidal`,
   `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `chart_isIso`, and K1's whole mate calculus
   are CLOSED (iter-031+).
The D3Рђ▓ Sq4 per-leg brick
`pullbackValIso_comp_leg` (the `pullbackValIso` composition coherence) is **CLOSED axiom-clean
(iter-019+)** Рђћ see below Рђћ so the ENTIRE D3Рђ▓ cone is sorry-free except the import-cycle-deferred
inverse.  It is proved by reducing to the sheaf-level cocycle `hH` (factoring out the leading
sheafification unit/forget via `comp_forget_cocycle`), then assembling `hH` from the inverted Sq1
(`sheafificationCompPullback_comp_inv`, via the generic `inv_telescope`), `pullbackComp` naturality,
`sheafificationCompPullback h` naturality, and the adjunction triangle `adj_unit_map_counit`, all
glued by the generic `cocycle_assemble` (every carrier-boundary `РЅФ` crossed by `exact`, never
`rw/erw [Category.assoc]`).
**`pullbackTensorMap_restrict` (D3Рђ▓) is now FULLY CLOSED (iter-019+).**
Its steps (i) [`comp_cancel_mid`+`exact`] and (ii) [`sheafifyMap_╬┤comp_split` ╬┤-split] were CLOSED
(iters-015/016), STEP (iii)a [`comp_slide_nested` `S1^h` slide] iter-017, (iii)b.1/(iii)b.2
[`comp_cancel_three_lr` + `comp_slide_three`/`map_comp_slide`] iter-018, and STEP (iii)b.3 Рђћ the
**folded Sq3/Sq4 presheaf core `hcore2`** Рђћ is CLOSED iter-018: fold each side into one `a_Z.map ╬е`
via `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_eq'` + the generic merge `map_comp4_eq_comp5`; `congr 1` to the presheaf
identity `╬е_L = ╬е_R`; close it by ╬┤-naturality of `╬┤_h` at `gg` (a CONCRETE `have h╬┤nat := ╬┤_natural`
spliced by `Рєљ reassoc_of%` Рђћ never `erw [reassoc_of% ╬┤_natural]`, which whnf-bombs on metavar
re-synthesis), bifunctoriality (the generic `tensorHom_collapse_3_4`), reducing to the two
structurally-identical per-leg `pullbackValIso` coherences `pullbackValIso_comp_leg h f M`/`Рђд N`.
That single brick (= blueprint `lem:pullback_val_iso_comp`; pVI-factorisation chase via the proven Sq1
`sheafificationCompPullback_comp` + counit naturality across `pullbackComp h f`) is the SOLE remaining
sorry of the whole D3Рђ▓ cone; its body has the `pullbackValIso`-unfold spliced as partial progress.
The D3Рђ▓ Sq1 sub-lemma
`sheafificationCompPullback_comp` is CLOSED (iter earlier).  **D1Рђ▓ (`pullbackTensorMap_natural`) is CLOSED
axiom-clean (iter-255)** via the mapin255 LIGHT `showРђдfrom` `╬┤_natural` `F`-ascription
(Sq2) plus the `.val`/`.obj` `erw`/`refine`-isDefEq Sq3/Sq4 assembly (see its proof
below).  **STEP A Рђћ the D1Рђ▓-helper
`sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_natural` Рђћ is CLOSED axiom-clean (iter-254)** via the tscmp254
`tensorHom`-pin: `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_eq'` states the comparison as ONE `a.map (╬и РіЌ ╬и)`
(single monoidal instance on the `РІЎ forgetРѓѓ` carrier), and the naturality reduces to
bifunctoriality (`tensorHom_comp_tensorHom`, applied as a defeq-matched TERM with explicit
`(C := Рђд)` to bridge the non-canonical instance) + the two single-component unit squares.
**D2Рђ▓ is CLOSED axiom-clean** (iter-250):
the unit-square `(РѕЌРѕЌ)` presheaf residual inside `pullbackEtaUnitSquare` is discharged,
so `pullbackEtaUnitSquare` Рєњ `pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso` are sorry-free
(`pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso` verified axiom-clean: only `propext`/`Classical.choice`/
`Quot.sound`). The `(РѕЌРѕЌ)` close is the assembly of three project lemmas Рђћ the Y-side
sheafification right-triangle `pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle`, the presheaf mate
`presheafUnit_comp_map_eta`, and the step-7 `╬х`-reconciliation `epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit`
(both sides act sectionwise as `¤є.hom.app X`) Рђћ after the substep-(i) `.val` reshaping and a
SYNTACTIC `restrictScalars (­ЮЪЎ)`-strip via the project lemma `restrictScalarsId_map` (stripping
`restrictScalars (­ЮЪЎ)` by `whnf`/`show` on the sheafification-laden composites is catastrophic;
the propositional rewrite + `erw` reassociation sidesteps it). The whole abstract mate-calculus
telescope (steps 1РђЊ6: `homEquiv` transposition, `compHomEquivFactor`/`leftAdjointUniqUnitEta` via
`hkey`, the two `homEquiv_naturality` folds, the X-side triangle `hXtri`, the X-side `homEquiv`
collapse `hrhs`) is upstream of the close. (The route-(e)
whiskering residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` was CLOSED iter-237 in
`Vestigial.lean`, so `tensorObj_assoc_iso` is now unconditional and axiom-clean.)
The dual-block is now
complete axiom-clean: the value layer (`InternalHom.internalHom`, `dual`,
`evalLin`/`internalHomEvalApp`) plus the evaluation morphism `internalHomEval`
(its naturality CLOSED iter-224, see below). The consumer `PicSharp.addCommGroup`
was rewired downstream in `RelPicFunctor.lean` (iter-247).

iter-224 on `internalHomEval`'s naturality: CLOSED axiom-clean. The iter-222/223 `whnf`
heartbeat-bomb diagnosis (the codomain `­ЮЪЎ_` forcing `kabstract` to whnf the monoidal-unit
machinery on the first rewrite) was STALE Рђћ a Mathlib update made the composition split cleanly
with `erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, Рђд]`, after which the six-step `evalLin`/`naturality_apply`/`hdt`
reduction goes through with no bomb, no `with_reducible`, and no `maxHeartbeats` bump.

The 2 blueprint-pinned declarations are:

1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (def) Рђћ the substrate binary
   operation `РіЌ : Scheme.Modules X ├Ќ Scheme.Modules X Рєњ Scheme.Modules X`,
   lifting `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` on underlying presheaves
   composed with sheafification on the small Zariski site.
   Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`.

2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality` (def) Рђћ the
   functorial action of `РіЌ` on morphisms: a pair `f : M РЪХ M'`, `g : N РЪХ N'`
   determines `f РіЌ g : tensorObj M N РЪХ tensorObj M' N'`.
   Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`.

(A full `MonoidalCategory (Scheme.Modules X)` instance is **deliberately not
built** Рђћ see ┬Д2 and blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`. The group
law on iso-classes consumes only the *existence* of the three coherence
isomorphisms, never a coherent monoidal category, so no such instance is on the
critical path.)

The consumer `tensorObjOnProduct` and the `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` stub now
live downstream in `RelPicFunctor.lean` (iter-247 import-cycle fix).

Plus (PUSH-BEYOND) the supporting helper lemmas of the lift section
(`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`,
`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`, `lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct`,
`lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj`).

## References

Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` (740 LOC,
4 pins). Source: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", ┬Д2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 ┬Д9.2),
Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`; Stacks tags 01CR (Picard group), 03DM (relative
tensor product of `O_X`-modules). Mathlib module
`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules`
(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`).

## Sub-module layout (iter-232 split)

The 2375-line monolith was split into sub-files under
`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/` (all imported by this file):

- `StalkTensor.lean` Рђћ the d.2 ingredient `stalkTensorIso` (`(AРіЌрхќB).stalk РЅЁ A.stalk РіЌ B.stalk`).
- `Vestigial.lean` Рђћ quarantined vestigial/route-(e) sections:
  `FlatWhisker`/`WhiskerOfW` (the route-(e) whisker sorry was CLOSED iter-237;
  `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` lives here), `StalkLinearMap`, `OverSliceSheafEquiv`.
- `PresheafInternalHom.lean` Рђћ foundational presheaf algebra + C-bridge substrate:
  `RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor`, lax-monoidal `restrictScalars`, pushforward
  adjunction (H1), `StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars` (H2), `InternalHom`, `Dual`.
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (this file) Рђћ public API:
  `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj`, unitors/braiding/assoc, `tensorObj_restrict_iso`,
  `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`, `homMk`.  (`exists_tensorObj_inverse` was MOVED to
  `TensorObjInverse.lean`, iter-023.)  `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial` is fully proved
  (body closed; transitively gated only on the K1 `pushforward_lax_mu_comparison_lhs_tmul` residual).
  Consumer `tensorObjOnProduct` and the `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` stub now live
  downstream in `RelPicFunctor.lean` (iter-247 import-cycle fix).
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Scheme

namespace Modules

/-! ## ┬Д1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/

/-- **The substrate operation `РіЌ` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**

For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
`M РіЌ_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M РіЌ_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) РіЌ_A N(Spec A)`).

Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)

/-- **Functoriality of `РіЌ_X`.**

A pair of morphisms `f : M РЪХ M'` and `g : N РЪХ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
morphism `f РіЌ g : tensorObj M N РЪХ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
and composition; the assignment `(M, N) Рєд tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
bifunctor `X.Modules ├Ќ X.Modules РЦц X.Modules` natural in both arguments.

Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
    (f : M РЪХ M') (g : N РЪХ N') : tensorObj M N РЪХ tensorObj M' N' :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)

/-- **`РіЌ`-invertibility of an `­Юњф_X`-module.** (Blueprint
`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `РіЌ`-invertible when it
admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M РіЌ_X N РЅЁ ­Юњф_X`, where
`­Юњф_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
law is definitional. -/
def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
  РѕЃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N РЅЁ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)

/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^Рѕе := РёІom_{­Юњф_X}(M, ­Юњф_X)`** of an `­Юњф_X`-module.

For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
`M^Рѕе(U) = РёІom_{­Юњф_X|_U}(M|_U, ­Юњф_X|_U)`).

Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (­ЮЪЎ Рђд)` on the
small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
exactly the base `RРѓђ : Dрхњрхќ РЦц CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
requires (the value `M^Рѕе(U) = M|_U РЪХ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
commutativity) Рђћ no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.

The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).

Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (┬Д`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
`РіЌ`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
      (PresheafOfModules.dual (RРѓђ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)

/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
`e : M РЅЁ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' РЅЁ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f РЅЁ ­Юњф` yields, contravariantly,
`dual ­Юњф РЅЁ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M РЅЁ M') :
    dual M' РЅЁ dual M :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (RРѓђ := X.presheaf)
      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))

/-! ## ┬Д2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure

iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
bundles Рђћ every group axiom is a `Nonempty (Рђд РЅЁ Рђд)` proposition, so no monoidal
coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules RРѓђ)` wall; they are removed here. The group
law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/

/-! ## ┬Д3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)

The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/

/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**

A pair of isomorphisms `e : M РЅЁ M'` and `e' : N РЅЁ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
isomorphism `tensorObj M N РЅЁ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
    (e : M РЅЁ M') (e' : N РЅЁ N') : tensorObj M N РЅЁ tensorObj M' N' :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat))
      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))

/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
sheaf.**

`tensorObj ­Юњф_X ­Юњф_X РЅЁ ­Юњф_X`, where `­Юњф_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
`╬╗_ (­ЮЪЎ_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
      РЅЁ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
      (╬╗_ (­ЮЪЎ_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat)))) РЅфРЅФ
    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)

/-- **Left unitor for `РіЌ_X`.** `­Юњф_X РіЌ_X M РЅЁ M`. (Blueprint
`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `╬╗_ M.val`, composed with the
sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M РЅЁ M :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) РЅфРЅФ
    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M

/-- **Right unitor for `РіЌ_X`.** `M РіЌ_X ­Юњф_X РЅЁ M`. (Blueprint
`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `¤Ђ_ M.val`, composed with the
sheafification counit. -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) РЅЁ M :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) РЅфРЅФ
    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M

/-- **Braiding for `РіЌ_X`.** `M РіЌ_X N РЅЁ N РіЌ_X M`. (Blueprint
`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `╬▓_ M.val
N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
    tensorObj M N РЅЁ tensorObj N M :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
    (BraidedCategory.braiding
      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)

/-- **Associator for `РіЌ_X`.** (Blueprint
`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
For arbitrary `M, N, P : X.Modules` there is an isomorphism
`(M РіЌ_X N) РіЌ_X P РЅЁ M РіЌ_X (N РіЌ_X P)`. This is the objectwise existence-of-iso datum the
group law consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (Рђд РЅЁ Рђд)`).

**UNCONDITIONAL and axiom-clean (iter-238 ROUTE (d)).** No flatness or local-triviality
hypothesis is used: the earlier flatness route (`W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_flat`, needing
sectionwise flatness Рђћ false for a general line bundle on a non-affine open) is RETIRED.
Writing `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)` and `╬и` the
sheafification-adjunction unit, the three-step composite is:
  1. `a(╬и_{M.val РіЌрхќ N.val} Рќи P.val)` is iso, giving
     `(M РіЌ N) РіЌ P РЅЁ a((M.valРіЌN.val) РіЌ P.val)`;
  2. `a.mapIso ╬▒ : a((M.valРіЌN.val)РіЌP.val) РЅЁ a(M.valРіЌ(N.valРіЌP.val))`, `╬▒` the
     presheaf-of-modules associator;
  3. `a(M.val РЌЂ ╬и_{N.val РіЌрхќ P.val})` is iso, giving
     `a(M.valРіЌ(N.valРіЌP.val)) РЅЁ M РіЌ (N РіЌ P)`.
Steps 1/3 invert the whiskered sheafification unit via the flatness-free
`PresheafOfModules.W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W` (╬и = `toSheafify Рѕѕ J.W`, and `J.W` is
stable under whiskering) together with `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (the sheafification
functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`). The defeq carrier bridge
`X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat` is handled by the leading
`letI instMS` below. -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules} :
    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P РЅЁ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) := by
  -- UNCONDITIONAL (iter-238, step 0): the locally-trivial hypotheses are dropped Рђћ
  -- the body never consumed them (the whiskered-unit localizer fact holds for
  -- arbitrary modules under ROUTE (d)). Matches the blueprint `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`
  -- framed unconditional and enables `tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible`.
  -- Bridge the monoidal structure across the `rfl`-defeq carrier
  -- `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat`.
  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat)))
  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
  -- Underlying presheaf tensors and the sheafification unit `╬и = toSheafify`.
  set MN := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val with hMN
  set NP := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) N.val P.val with hNP
  set ╬и := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with h╬и
  -- The two whiskered-unit localizer facts (ROUTE (d), via `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`).
  -- `╬и_A = toSheafify` lies in `J.W` (`W_toSheafify`).
  have h╬иMN : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (╬и.app MN)) := by
    rw [h╬и, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
  have h╬иNP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (╬и.app NP)) := by
    rw [h╬и, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (╬и.app MN Рќи P.val)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) P.val (╬и.app MN) h╬иMN
  have hW3 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (M.val РЌЂ ╬и.app NP)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) M.val (╬и.app NP) h╬иNP
  -- Steps 1 and 3: the sheafification functor inverts the whiskered units.
  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (╬и.app MN Рќи P.val)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
  have hi3 : IsIso (a.map (M.val РЌЂ ╬и.app NP)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW3
  -- Step 2: the presheaf-of-modules associator, transported under `a`.
  have e2 := a.mapIso
    ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).associator M.val N.val P.val)
  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1).symm РЅфРЅФ e2 РЅфРЅФ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi3)

/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
open `U` (`M.restrict U.╬╣ РЅЁ ­Юњф_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W РЅц U`.

The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
factor `W.╬╣ = (X.homOfLE hWU) РЅФ U.╬╣`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.╬╣` with
`(M.restrict U.╬╣).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
    (hWU : W РЅц U)
    (e : M.restrict U.╬╣ РЅЁ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
    M.restrict W.╬╣ РЅЁ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
  have hWU' : W РЅц (­ЮЪЎ X) РЂ╗┬╣рхЂ U := hWU
  set j : (W : Scheme) РЪХ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (­ЮЪЎ X) U W hWU' with hj
  have hj╬╣ : j РЅФ U.╬╣ = W.╬╣ := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_╬╣, Category.comp_id]
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
  -- M.restrict W.╬╣ РЅЁ (pullback W.╬╣).obj M
  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.╬╣).app M РЅфРЅФ ?_
  -- РЅЁ (pullback (j РЅФ U.╬╣)).obj M
  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hj╬╣.symm).app M РЅфРЅФ ?_
  -- РЅЁ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.╬╣).obj M)
  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.╬╣).symm.app M РЅфРЅФ ?_
  -- РЅЁ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.╬╣)
  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.╬╣).symm.app M) РЅфРЅФ ?_
  -- РЅЁ (pullback j).obj ­Юњф_U
  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e РЅфРЅФ ?_
  -- РЅЁ ­Юњф_W
  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI

/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**

For an open immersion `f : Y РЪХ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
`(tensorObj M N).restrict f РЅЁ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.

This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` Рђћ **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
(iter-217). It says the substrate `РіЌ` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
is the blueprint's four-step composite:
  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
    `(pullback ¤є).obj (M.val РіЌРѓџ N.val) РЅЁ (M.restrict f).val РіЌРѓџ (N.restrict f).val`.
  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 Рѕў H2**:
    Рђб H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
      `pushforward ╬▓ РЅЁ pullback ¤є`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
      pair `f.opensFunctor РіБ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `╬▓` is the
      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward ╬▓).obj M.val`
      definitionally.
    Рђб H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward ╬▓ = pushforwardРѓђ РІЎ restrictScalars ╬▓`
      with `╬▓` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars ╬▓`
      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{╬╝,╬х}`); the composite
      `╬╝Iso` is the tensorator.
The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y РЪХ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
    (tensorObj M N).restrict f РЅЁ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) РЅфРЅФ ?_
  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
  -- `sheafification РІЎ pullback ¤є РЅЁ PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є.hom РІЎ
  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є.hom).obj (M.val РіЌ N.val))
  --    РЅЁ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) РЅфРЅФ ?_
  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (­ЮЪЎ Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback ¤є.hom).obj (M.val РіЌРѓџ N.val)`, and
  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
  -- `(M.restrict f).val РіЌРѓџ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE Рђћ iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є).obj (M.val РіЌРѓџ N.val)
  --      РЅЁ (M.restrict f).val РіЌРѓџ (N.restrict f).val`
  -- where `¤є = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `РіЌРѓџ =
  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward ╬▓ РЅЁ pullback ¤є`, built from
  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `╬▓` is the
  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward ╬▓).obj M.val` definitionally.
  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward ╬▓).obj (A РіЌРѓџ B) РЅЁ
  --      (pushforward ╬▓).obj A РіЌРѓџ (pushforward ╬▓).obj B`.
  -- `¤єR` (the scheme structure map) and `╬▓` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
  let ¤єR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `╬▓` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward ╬▓).obj M.val`).
  let ╬▒ : Y.presheaf РЪХ f.opensFunctor.op РІЎ X.presheaf :=
    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  let ╬▓ : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj РЪХ f.opensFunctor.op РІЎ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
    Functor.whiskerRight ╬▒ (forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat)
  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward ╬▓ РіБ PresheafOfModules.pushforward ¤єR :=
    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction ╬▓ ¤єR
      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction ¤єR)
  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).symm РЅфРЅФ ?_
  -- H2: the strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward ╬▓ = pushforwardРѓђ РІЎ restrictScalars ╬▓`.
  -- `╬▓` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forgetРѓѓ`-image of the open-immersion structure ring
  -- ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars ╬▓` is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`),
  -- and `pushforwardРѓђOfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib); the composite's `╬╝Iso` is the tensorator.
  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is
  -- found canonically); the result is DEFEQ to the goal Рђћ whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only
  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ` Рђћ and `(pushforward ╬▓).obj M.val =
  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.
  have h╬▓ : Рѕђ U, Function.Bijective (╬▓.app U).hom := by
    intro U
    haveI : IsIso (╬▓.app U) :=
      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))
    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (╬▓.app U)
  let ╬▓' : (Y.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat) РЪХ
      (f.opensFunctor.op РІЎ X.presheaf) РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat := ╬▓
  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars ╬▓').Monoidal :=
    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective ╬▓' h╬▓
  exact (Functor.Monoidal.╬╝Iso
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforwardРѓђOfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf
      РІЎ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars ╬▓')
    (M.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat))
    (N.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat))).symm

/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**

If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
a common affine open `W РѕІ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
`(M РіЌ N)|_W РЅЁ M|_W РіЌ N|_W РЅЁ ­Юњф_W РіЌ ­Юњф_W РЅЁ ­Юњф_W`. The only residual gap is the
substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
  intro x
  obtain РЪеU, hxU, hU_aff, РЪеeMРЪЕРЪЕ := hM x
  obtain РЪеU', hxU', hU'_aff, РЪеeNРЪЕРЪЕ := hN x
  obtain РЪеW, hW_aff, hxW, hWsubРЪЕ :=
    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U РіЊ U') РЪеhxU, hxU'РЪЕ
  have hWU : W РЅц U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
  have hWU' : W РЅц U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
  refine РЪеW, hxW, hW_aff, РЪе?_РЪЕРЪЕ
  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.╬╣ M N РЅфРЅФ
    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) РЅфРЅФ
    tensorObj_unit_iso

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement Рђћ the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)

The "locally-iso РЄњ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
a morphism of `­Юњф_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-РіЌ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/

/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `­Юњф_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `¤є : M РЪХ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).╬╣).map ¤є`
is an isomorphism, then `¤є` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (¤є : M РЪХ N)
    (U : X Рєњ X.Opens) (hxU : Рѕђ x, x Рѕѕ U x)
    (h : Рѕђ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).╬╣).map ¤є)) :
    IsIso ¤є := by
  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
  have hst : Рѕђ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map ¤є)) := by
    intro x
    obtain РЪеx', hx'РЪЕ : РѕЃ x', (U x).╬╣ x' = x := by
      have hmem : x Рѕѕ (U x).╬╣.opensRange := by
        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_╬╣]; exact hxU x
      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).╬╣).map ¤є) := h x
    -- `(restrictFunctor Рђд РІЎ toPresheaf Рђд РІЎ stalkFunctor x').map ¤є` is iso (functor of an iso).
    haveI hF¤є : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).╬╣ РІЎ
        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ РІЎ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map ¤є) := by
      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).╬╣ x' = x`.
    have hG¤є : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).╬╣ x')).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map ¤є)) :=
      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).╬╣ x') ¤є).mp hF¤є
    exact hx' РќИ hG¤є
  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := РЪеM.presheaf, M.isSheafРЪЕ
  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := РЪеN.presheaf, N.isSheafРЪЕ
  let fS : MS РЪХ NS := РЪе(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map ¤єРЪЕ
  haveI : Рѕђ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := hst
  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map ¤є) := by
    have := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
    exact this
  exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso ¤є (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1

/-- **A-bridge step (ii): promote an `­Юњф_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module
morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf РЪХ N.presheaf` of the underlying
`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `­Юњф_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M РЪХ N`
of `­Юњф_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
it is the "promote to `­Юњф_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
    (g : M.val.presheaf РЪХ N.val.presheaf)
    (hg : Рѕђ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)рхњрхќ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
      (g.app V).hom (r Рђб m) = r Рђб (g.app V).hom m) :
    M РЪХ N :=
  РЪеPresheafOfModules.homMk (MРѓЂ := M.val) (MРѓѓ := N.val) g hgРЪЕ

/-! ## ┬Д5. The invertibility-carrier Picard group `picCommGroup`

This is the by-hand commutative-group law on isomorphism classes of `РіЌ`-invertible
`­Юњф_X`-modules (blueprint ┬Д`sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier`). Every group axiom is a single
existence-of-isomorphism `Nonempty (Рђд РЅЁ Рђд)` read as an equality of iso-classes; no
pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherence and no `MonoidalCategory` instance is invoked.
The inverse is carried by the membership witness of `IsInvertible` itself. -/

/-- **Step 1 Рђћ associator on `РіЌ`-invertible objects** (blueprint
`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso_invertible`). An immediate specialisation of the now
*unconditional* `tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the invertibility hypotheses are not consumed
(they match the blueprint statement). -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules}
    (_hM : IsInvertible M) (_hN : IsInvertible N) (_hP : IsInvertible P) :
    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P РЅЁ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) :=
  tensorObj_assoc_iso

/-- **Middle-four interchange for `РіЌ_X`** (helper). For arbitrary `­Юњф_X`-modules
`A, B, C, D`, there is an isomorphism `(A РіЌ B) РіЌ (C РіЌ D) РЅЁ (A РіЌ C) РіЌ (B РіЌ D)`,
assembled from the unconditional associator and the braiding (no coherence consumed).
Used to reassociate the four factors in `IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
private noncomputable def tensorObj_middleFour {X : Scheme.{u}} (A B C D : X.Modules) :
    tensorObj (tensorObj A B) (tensorObj C D)
      РЅЁ tensorObj (tensorObj A C) (tensorObj B D) :=
  tensorObj_assoc_iso РЅфРЅФ
    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl A)
      (tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm РЅфРЅФ
        tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding B C) (Iso.refl D) РЅфРЅФ
        tensorObj_assoc_iso) РЅфРЅФ
    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm

/-- **Step 3 Рђћ `РіЌ`-invertibility is closed under tensor product** (blueprint
`lem:isinvertible_tensor`). If `M, M'` are `РіЌ`-invertible with inverses `N, N'`,
then `N РіЌ N'` is a tensor inverse of `M РіЌ M'`. -/
theorem IsInvertible.tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules}
    (hM : IsInvertible M) (hM' : IsInvertible M') :
    IsInvertible (Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M M') := by
  obtain РЪеN, РЪеeРЪЕРЪЕ := hM
  obtain РЪеN', РЪеe'РЪЕРЪЕ := hM'
  exact РЪеScheme.Modules.tensorObj N N',
    РЪеtensorObj_middleFour M M' N N' РЅфРЅФ tensorObjIsoOfIso e e' РЅфРЅФ tensorObj_unit_isoРЪЕРЪЕ

/-- **Step 4 Рђћ the structure sheaf is `РіЌ`-invertible** (blueprint
`lem:isinvertible_unit`). Witness `­Юњф_X`, iso `tensorObj_unit_iso`. -/
theorem isInvertible_unit {X : Scheme.{u}} :
    IsInvertible (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
  РЪеSheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, РЪеtensorObj_unit_isoРЪЕРЪЕ

/-- **Step 5 Рђћ the tensor inverse is determined up to isomorphism** (blueprint
`lem:isinvertible_inverse_welldef`). If `M РіЌ N РЅЁ ­Юњф_X` and `M РіЌ N' РЅЁ ­Юњф_X` then
`N РЅЁ N'`, via the inverse-of-inverse chain. -/
theorem IsInvertible.inverse_unique {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N N' : X.Modules}
    (e : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N РЅЁ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
    (e' : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N' РЅЁ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
    Nonempty (N РЅЁ N') :=
  РЪе(tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm РЅфРЅФ
    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm РЅфРЅФ
    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm РЅфРЅФ
    tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding N M РЅфРЅФ e) (Iso.refl N') РЅфРЅФ
    tensorObj_left_unitor N'РЪЕ

/-- The setoid of `РіЌ`-invertible `­Юњф_X`-modules: `M Рѕ╝ M'` iff there exists an
isomorphism `M РЅЁ M'` (blueprint `def:pic_carrier`). -/
instance picSetoid (X : Scheme.{u}) : Setoid {M : X.Modules // IsInvertible M} where
  r M M' := Nonempty ((M : X.Modules) РЅЁ (M' : X.Modules))
  iseqv :=
    РЪеfun _ => РЪеIso.refl _РЪЕ, fun РЪеeРЪЕ => РЪеe.symmРЪЕ, fun РЪеeРЪЕ РЪеfРЪЕ => РЪеe РЅфРЅФ fРЪЕРЪЕ

/-- **Step 2 Рђћ the invertibility-carrier Picard group** (blueprint
`def:pic_carrier`): the quotient of `РіЌ`-invertible `­Юњф_X`-modules by isomorphism. -/
def PicGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := Quotient (picSetoid X)

/-- Multiplication on `PicGroup X`: `[M] ┬и [M'] := [M РіЌ_X M']`, well-defined on
iso-classes by bifunctoriality (`tensorObjIsoOfIso`), landing in `PicGroup` by
`IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
noncomputable def picMul {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X Рєњ PicGroup X Рєњ PicGroup X :=
  Quotient.liftРѓѓ
    (fun a b => Quotient.mk _ РЪеtensorObj a.1 b.1, a.2.tensorObj b.2РЪЕ)
    (by
      rintro РЪеa, haРЪЕ РЪеb, hbРЪЕ РЪеa', ha'РЪЕ РЪеb', hb'РЪЕ РЪеeaРЪЕ РЪеebРЪЕ
      exact Quotient.sound РЪеtensorObjIsoOfIso ea ebРЪЕ)

/-- The inverse class on `PicGroup X`: `[M] Рєд [N]` for the membership witness `N`
of `IsInvertible M`, well-defined by `IsInvertible.inverse_unique`. -/
noncomputable def picInv {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X Рєњ PicGroup X :=
  Quotient.lift
    (fun a => Quotient.mk _
      РЪеClassical.choose a.2,
        a.1, РЪеtensorObj_braiding _ a.1 РЅфРЅФ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).someРЪЕРЪЕ)
    (by
      rintro РЪеa, haРЪЕ РЪеa', ha'РЪЕ РЪеeaРЪЕ
      refine Quotient.sound ?_
      -- both `Classical.choose ha` and `Classical.choose ha'` are tensor inverses of `a`
      have h1 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha) РЅЁ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
        (Classical.choose_spec ha).some
      have h2 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha') РЅЁ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
        tensorObjIsoOfIso ea (Iso.refl _) РЅфРЅФ (Classical.choose_spec ha').some
      exact IsInvertible.inverse_unique h1 h2)

/-- **Step 6 Рђћ the invertibility-carrier Picard group is abelian** (blueprint
`thm:pic_commgroup`). `[M] ┬и [M'] := [M РіЌ_X M']`, `1 := [­Юњф_X]`, and `[M]РЂ╗┬╣` the
class of any membership witness of `IsInvertible M`. Each group axiom is a single
existence-of-isomorphism: unit laws Рєљ unitors, associativity Рєљ associator,
commutativity Рєљ braiding, left inverse Рєљ the witness iso. No monoidal coherence. -/
noncomputable instance picCommGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : CommGroup (PicGroup X) where
  mul := picMul
  one := Quotient.mk _ РЪеSheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, isInvertible_unitРЪЕ
  inv := picInv
  mul_assoc := by
    rintro a b c
    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
    induction c using Quotient.ind with | _ c => ?_
    exact Quotient.sound РЪеtensorObj_assoc_isoРЪЕ
  one_mul := by
    rintro a
    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
    exact Quotient.sound РЪеtensorObj_left_unitor a.1РЪЕ
  mul_one := by
    rintro a
    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
    exact Quotient.sound РЪеtensorObj_right_unitor a.1РЪЕ
  inv_mul_cancel := by
    rintro a
    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
    exact Quotient.sound
      РЪеtensorObj_braiding (Classical.choose a.2) a.1 РЅфРЅФ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).someРЪЕ
  mul_comm := by
    rintro a b
    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
    exact Quotient.sound РЪеtensorObj_braiding a.1 b.1РЪЕ

/-! ## ┬Д6. Pullback-monoidality substrate (A.1.c): `IsInvertible.pullback`

Project-local Mathlib supplement. The relative Picard consumer re-bases onto the
`IsInvertible` carrier and its structure maps are *pullback* maps for GENERAL scheme
morphisms (the projection `C ├Ќ_S T Рєњ T` and base-change maps are neither open
immersions nor flat). We need that pullback preserves `РіЌ`-invertibility. This requires
`pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M РіЌ N) РЅЁ f^*M РіЌ f^*N`) and `pullbackUnitIso`
(`f^*­Юњф_X РЅЁ ­Юњф_Y`). Blueprint ┬Д`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`. -/

/-- **Composition coherence of the unitРєњpushforward-unit comparison.**

For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z РЪХ Y`, `f : Y РЪХ X`, the canonical comparison
`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` of the composite `h РЅФ f` factors through the comparisons
of `f` and `h` and the pushforward pseudofunctor coherence `pushforwardComp`. This is
the *pushforward-side* (right-adjoint) half of the composition coherence; it is
concrete (sectionwise it is just functoriality of the structure-sheaf ring maps,
hence `rfl` after the `ext`-chain) and is the pushforward-side input from which the
genuinely-needed *pullback-side* coherence of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is obtained by
adjunction-mate transport. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit. -/
lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z РЪХ Y) (f : Y РЪХ X) :
    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h РЅФ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
      SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom РЅФ
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) РЅФ
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf) := by
  apply SheafOfModules.Hom.ext
  apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
  intro U
  apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
  apply LinearMap.ext
  intro a
  rfl

/-- **Composition coherence of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
(the genuinely-new ingredient for `pullbackUnitIso`).**

For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z РЪХ Y`, `f : Y РЪХ X`, the canonical comparison
`f^*­Юњф РЪХ ­Юњф` of the composite `h РЅФ f` factors through the comparisons of `f` and `h`
and the pullback pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackComp`:
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit (hРЅФf) = (pullbackComp h f).inv РЅФ (pullback h).map (pbu f) РЅФ pbu h`.

This is the pullback-side (left-adjoint) composition coherence Рђћ Mathlib-absent at the
pinned commit and NOT a sectionwise statement (the abstract left-adjoint pullback has no
sectionwise value). It is obtained by adjunction-mate transport from the pushforward-side
coherence `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp`: transposing both sides under
`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (hРЅФf)`, the left side becomes `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (hРЅФf)`
and the right side is reassembled via the conjugate/mate identity
`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` (relating `pullbackComp.inv` to `pushforwardComp.hom`),
`unit_conjugateEquiv`, and the composite-adjunction unit `Adjunction.comp_unit_app`.

Consumed by `pullbackUnitIso`: on an affine chart `V` the inclusion `V.╬╣ РЅФ f` factors as
`g РЅФ U.╬╣` with `Opens.map g.base` `Final`, so two applications of this coherence (one for
each factorisation) express the restricted global comparison as a composite of isomorphisms
(`pbu` for an open immersion / a `Final`-chart morphism is an iso), whence
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an iso by `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`.

The proof uses `erw` for the associativity / functoriality steps because the `SheafOfModules`
category compositions appear in defeq-but-not-syntactic forms (`Scheme.Modules.pullback f`
vs `SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`) on which plain `rw [Category.assoc]` /
`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fail to unify. -/
lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z РЪХ Y) (f : Y РЪХ X) :
    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h РЅФ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) РЅФ
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) РЅФ
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
  have key := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp h f
  have conj := unit_conjugateEquiv
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h))
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h РЅФ f))
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at conj
  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h РЅФ f)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
  have hL : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h РЅФ f)).homEquiv _ _
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h РЅФ f).toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h РЅФ f).toRingCatSheafHom := by
    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
      (h РЅФ f).toRingCatSheafHom
  have hi : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h РЅФ f)).homEquiv _ _
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
          (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) РЅФ
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f РІЎ Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj
            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := by
    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]; exact conj.symm
  have hLf : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom := by
    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
      f.toRingCatSheafHom
  have hLh : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
      h.toRingCatSheafHom
  have hinner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).unit.app
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) РЅФ
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) РЅФ
          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom РЅФ
        SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
    have e := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)
      (X := (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
      (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf)
      (f := (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) РЅФ
        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    have key2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
              (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) РЅФ
            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom РЅФ
            SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
      rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left]; exact congrArg _ hLh
    exact e.symm.trans key2
  have hcomp' : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) РЅФ
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h РІЎ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) РЅФ
          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom РЅФ
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    have ef := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
      (X := SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)
      (f := SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
    rw [Adjunction.comp_unit_app, Functor.comp_map]
    erw [Category.assoc, Рєљ Functor.map_comp, hinner, Functor.map_comp]
    erw [Рєљ Category.assoc]
    rw [show (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) РЅФ
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
        = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom from ef.symm.trans hLf]
    rfl
  rw [hL, key, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, hi]
  erw [Category.assoc, Рєљ (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.naturality
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) РЅФ
        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)]
  erw [Рєљ Category.assoc, hcomp']
  erw [Category.assoc]

/-! ### Phase 1 Рђћ `pullbackUnitIso` (`f^*­Юњф_X РЅЁ ­Юњф_Y`), blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`

**iter-241 RESOLUTION (the chart-chase is NOT needed).** The unit comparison
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for *every* morphism of
schemes `f`, not just for `Final`-chart morphisms. The Mathlib instance
`SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal` fires whenever the comparison
functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`, and that functor is **always** `Final`: the
preimage functor on opens preserves finite limits (it is a frame homomorphism), so it is
representably flat, whence `final_of_representablyFlat` supplies `(Opens.map f.base).Final`
unconditionally. (Verified axiom-clean for a general `f`.) The elaborate affine
chart-chase contemplated by the blueprint proof Рђћ and the iter-240 coherence linchpin
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` Рђћ are therefore unnecessary for this lemma (the linchpin is
retained above as it is the genuine Mathlib-absent pseudofunctor coherence, of independent
use for the harder Phase-2 tensor comparison).

The remaining friction was purely a Lean typeclass-resolution accident: in a context with
several `(Opens.map _).Final` hypotheses (or after a `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite)
the buried implicit instance args of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (`[F.IsContinuous]`,
`[(pushforward ¤є).IsRightAdjoint]`) are defeq-but-not-syntactic, so `asIso`/`infer_instance`
fails to synthesise `IsIso (pbu f)`. The fix (mathlib-analogist `pbu-canon`, the
`Functor.Monoidal.╬╝Iso` idiom): isolate the single `Final` hypothesis in the helper
`isIso_pbu_of_final` whose body `inferInstance` runs at a clean site, then transport the
resulting witness through `asIso` by passing it *explicitly* (`@asIso Рђд (isIso_pbu_of_final g)`)
Рђћ the application's defeq check runs at default transparency and succeeds, whereas instance
synthesis (reducible transparency) does not. -/

/-- **`IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit g)` from a single `Final` hypothesis, at a clean site.**
Project-local: isolates the lone `(Opens.map g.base).Final` instance so that the Mathlib
`OfFinal` instance synthesises without colliding with other in-scope `Final`/`IsIso`
hypotheses (see the section note). -/
private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y РЪХ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance

/-- **Bundled `Iso` form of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g`** for a `Final`
comparison functor Рђћ the analogue of `CategoryTheory.Functor.Monoidal.╬╝Iso`. Project-local:
hands out the isomorphism (rather than a bare `IsIso` instance) so downstream coherence
reasoning stays at the `Iso` level and never re-triggers the `pbu` instance synthesis. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y РЪХ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) РЅЁ
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  @asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
    (isIso_pbu_of_final g)

/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf** (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`):
`f^*­Юњф_X РЅЁ ­Юњф_Y` for an arbitrary morphism of schemes `f : Y РЪХ X`, where
`­Юњф_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`. The comparison functor `Opens.map f.base` is
always `Final` (preimage on opens is representably flat), so the Mathlib unit comparison
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism unconditionally. -/
noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y РЪХ X) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) РЅЁ
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f

/-- **Sheafification reconciles the presheaf tensor with the tensor of the
sheafified factors.** For presheaves of modules `P, Q` on `X`, sheafifying the
presheaf tensor `P РіЌ Q` agrees with sheafifying the tensor of the underlying
presheaves of their sheafifications `(a P).val РіЌ (a Q).val`, where
`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (­ЮЪЎ ­Юњф_X)`. This is the "sheafification is
monoidal" reconciliation, built Рђћ exactly as in `tensorObj_assoc_iso` Рђћ by
whiskering the sheafification unit `╬и` (a `J.W`-morphism, hence locally bijective)
on each side and inverting under `a` via `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` together
with the flatness-free `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`. It is the bridge reconciling a
presheaf-level tensorator with the substrate `РіЌ_X` (whose `.val` carries an extra
sheafification on each factor), as needed by the pullback-monoidality comparison
`pullbackTensorIso`. -/
noncomputable def sheafifyTensorUnitIso {X : Scheme.{u}}
    (P Q : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) P Q) РЅЁ
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf)
          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P).val
          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj Q).val) := by
  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat)))
  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
  set ╬и := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with h╬и
  have h╬иP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (╬и.app P)) := by
    rw [h╬и, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
  have h╬иQ : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (╬и.app Q)) := by
    rw [h╬и, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (╬и.app P Рќи Q)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) Q (╬и.app P) h╬иP
  have hW2 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map ((a.obj P).val РЌЂ ╬и.app Q)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) (a.obj P).val (╬и.app Q) h╬иQ
  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (╬и.app P Рќи Q)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
  have hi2 : IsIso (a.map ((a.obj P).val РЌЂ ╬и.app Q)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW2
  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1) РЅфРЅФ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi2)

/-- **The presheaf-of-modules pushforward is lax monoidal** (the analogist-named `╬╝_G`,
Mathlib-absent at the pin). For a morphism `¤є : SРѓђ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ РЪХ F.op РІЎ (RРѓђ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ)`
of presheaves of *commutative* rings, `PresheafOfModules.pushforward ¤є` unfolds to
`pushforwardРѓђOfCommRingCat F RРѓђ РІЎ restrictScalars ¤є`, the composite of the strong-monoidal
topological pushforward `pushforwardРѓђOfCommRingCat` (Mathlib) and the lax-monoidal
`restrictScalars ¤є` (project `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`), hence lax monoidal by
`Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`.

The hypothesis necessarily uses the *inner* `forgetРѓѓ` association (`F.op РІЎ (RРѓђ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ)`,
the form `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` expects), but the monoidal-category instance on the
middle presheaf is keyed on the *outer* association `(F.op РІЎ RРѓђ) РІЎ forgetРѓѓ` (the form
`pushforwardРѓђOfCommRingCat`'s target carries). The two are defeq; bridging them with a local
`MonoidalCategory` instance triggers a kernel-rejected diamond, so instead `¤є` is defeq-cast
to the outer form (`¤є'`) for the `restrictScalars` factor, and the resulting composite Рђћ defeq
to `pushforward ¤є` Рђћ is transported back by `exact`. This is the right-adjoint lax structure
that an eventual oplax comparison `╬┤` on the abstract left-adjoint pullback would inherit.
Project-local supplement; reusable for the general pullback-monoidality build. -/
noncomputable instance presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal
    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C РЦц D}
    {RРѓђ : Dрхњрхќ РЦц CommRingCat.{u}} {SРѓђ : Cрхњрхќ РЦц CommRingCat.{u}}
    (¤є : (SРѓђ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat) РЪХ
      F.op РІЎ (RРѓђ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward ¤є).LaxMonoidal := by
  let ¤є' : (SРѓђ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat) РЪХ
      (F.op РІЎ RРѓђ) РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat := ¤є
  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforwardРѓђOfCommRingCat F RРѓђ РІЎ
      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars ¤є').LaxMonoidal := inferInstance
  exact h

/-- **The abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback is oplax monoidal**, with canonical
comparison map `╬┤_{A,B} : f^*(A РіЌ B) РЪХ f^*A РіЌ f^*B`. This is the mate of the lax
tensorator of `pushforward ¤є` (`presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal`) across the
pullbackРђЊpushforward adjunction, via Mathlib's doctrinal `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`. It
supplies the canonical comparison map that the eventual `pullbackTensorIso` upgrades to an
isomorphism Рђћ note the map exists for the *abstract* left adjoint with no sectionwise value
(no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules)` is needed, contra the earlier reading: the comparison
lives at the presheaf level where `PresheafOfModules` IS monoidal). Project-local supplement;
what remains Mathlib-absent is the concrete inverse-image model needed to prove `╬┤` is an
iso (see the Phase-2 note below). -/
noncomputable instance presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal
    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C РЦц D}
    {RРѓђ : Dрхњрхќ РЦц CommRingCat.{u}} {SРѓђ : Cрхњрхќ РЦц CommRingCat.{u}}
    (¤є : (SРѓђ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat) РЪХ
      F.op РІЎ (RРѓђ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat))
    [(PresheafOfModules.pushforward ¤є).IsRightAdjoint] :
    (PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є).OplaxMonoidal :=
  (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction ¤є).leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal

/-! ### Phase 2 Рђћ `pullbackTensorIso` status (iter-242 finding)

`pullbackUnitIso` (Phase 1) is DONE (above). The remaining `pullbackTensorIso`
(`f^*(M РіЌ N) РЅЁ f^*M РіЌ f^*N`, general `f`) is a genuine Mathlib-scale build, blocked
on a Mathlib-absent ingredient confirmed this iter:

  Рђб `Scheme.Modules.pullback f` and the underlying `PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є.hom`
    are BOTH `(pushforward _).leftAdjoint` Рђћ an ABSTRACT left adjoint with no sectionwise
    value. The blueprint route "build a concrete strong-monoidal `P` РЅЁ pullback, then
    transport via `leftAdjointUniq`" requires a CONCRETE model `P` of the pullback.
  Рђб For an OPEN immersion (`tensorObj_restrict_iso`) the concrete model was
    `pushforward ╬▓` (╬▓ = the structure ring ISO), strong-monoidal via
    `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`. For a GENERAL `f` the concrete left adjoint is
    the inverse-image `(inverse-image presheaf) РІЎ extendScalars`: the underlying-presheaf
    inverse image is a LEFT KAN EXTENSION along `(Opens.map f.base).op` (a colimit, NOT
    sectionwise), and neither `PresheafOfModules.extendScalars` nor a concrete
    inverse-image-of-presheaves-of-modules functor exists in Mathlib at the pin. Building
    that concrete strong-monoidal functor + its adjunction to `pushforward` is the genuine
    multi-hundred-LOC obligation (Mathlib-scale; the `distribBaseChange` strong-monoidal
    core exists only at `ModuleCat.extendScalars`, the topological inverse image does not).

The reusable presheaf-level prerequisites toward that build are supplied just below
(`PresheafOfModules.pushforward` is LAX monoidal; hence the abstract presheaf pullback is
OPLAX monoidal with a canonical comparison map `╬┤`).

**SUPERSEDED (iter-243 pivot, see ┬ДD1'РђЊD4' below).** The general-`f` concrete-model route is
ABANDONED and off-path. The relative Picard consumer only ever needs `╬┤` to be an iso on LINE
BUNDLES, and there iso-ness comes via the **local-trivialisation chart-chase** (D2'РђЊD4'), NOT via a
concrete inverse-image model: the oplax `╬┤` is sheafified (`pullbackTensorMap`), reduced to the
single sheafified-presheaf comparison (`isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`), shown iso
on the unit pair (D2'), and globalised over a trivialising cover (D4', `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`).
The "no free oplax РЄњ preserves invertibles" obstruction (`╬Њ(РёЎ┬╣,­Юњф(1)) = 0`) is real for a GENERAL
module but is sidestepped for line bundles by the chart-chase Рђћ no concrete model is built. -/

/-- **Identifying the sheafified presheaf-pullback of `M.val` with the abstract pullback.**
For `f : Y РЪХ X` and `M : X.Modules`, sheafifying the presheaf-level pullback of the
underlying presheaf `M.val` recovers the abstract `­Юњф`-module pullback `(pullback f).obj M`.
This is the per-object form of `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` composed with the
sheafification counit on the (already-sheaf) `M`; it is the bridge used to reconcile the
right-hand side of the pullbackРђЊtensor comparison `pullbackTensorMap` with the substrate
`tensorObj` of the pullbacks. -/
noncomputable def pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y РЪХ X) (M : X.Modules) :
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom).obj M.val)
      РЅЁ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M :=
  ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app M.val).symm РЅфРЅФ
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapIso
      ((asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
        (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M)

/-- **The sheaf-level pullbackРђЊtensor comparison map `╬┤_sheaf`** (blueprint
`lem:pullback_tensor_map`). For a morphism of schemes `f : Y РЪХ X` and arbitrary
`M N : X.Modules`, the canonical comparison morphism
`f^*(M РіЌ_X N) РЪХ f^*M РіЌ_Y f^*N`. It is the sheaf-level transport of the presheaf-level
oplax comparison `╬┤` (`presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal`) through sheafification, assembled
from the `sheafificationCompPullback` device, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`, and `pullbackValIso`.
This is a *map only*: in general it is not asserted to be an isomorphism (the invertible
case is upgraded to an iso by local trivialisation in `IsInvertible.pullback`). -/
noncomputable def pullbackTensorMap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y РЪХ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (tensorObj M N) РЪХ
      tensorObj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj N) := by
  let ¤є := f.toRingCatSheafHom
  let ¤є' : (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat) РЪХ
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op РІЎ (Y.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat) := ¤є.hom
  let a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ Y.ringCatSheaf.val)
  refine ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback ¤є).app
      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).hom РЅФ ?_
  refine a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.╬┤ (PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є') M.val N.val) РЅФ ?_
  refine (sheafifyTensorUnitIso (X := Y)
      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є').obj M.val)
      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є').obj N.val)).hom РЅФ ?_
  exact a_Y.map (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
    (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat))
    ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
    ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement Рђћ D1: the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition

These are general `PresheafOfModules` statements (any `F : C РЦц D`, ring presheaves
`R, S`), not specific to schemes; they are project-local because Mathlib's pinned commit
exposes neither `extendScalars` nor `pullbackРѓђ` at the presheaf-of-modules level. Both are
realised as the left adjoint of an existing right adjoint: `pushforwardРѓђ F R` is
definitionally `pushforward (­ЮЪЎ (F.op РІЎ R))` (because `restrictScalars (­ЮЪЎ) = ­ЮЪГ` on the nose,
witnessed by Mathlib's `restrictScalars (­ЮЪЎ R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (­ЮЪГ _).Full`), and
`restrictScalars ¤є` is definitionally `pushforward (F := ­ЮЪГ) ¤є`; since `pushforward _` is
always a right adjoint, so are these two, and their left adjoints `pullbackРѓђ`/`extendScalars`
exist. The decomposition `pullback ¤є РЅЁ extendScalars ¤є РІЎ pullbackРѓђ` then follows from the
definitional factorisation `pushforward ¤є = pushforwardРѓђ F R РІЎ restrictScalars ¤є` by
uniqueness of left adjoints (`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso`).

Per blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition` (D1). **OFF-PATH (iter-243 pivot).** D1 is
axiom-clean and self-contained, but it was the first brick of the ABANDONED general
strong-monoidal pullback build (`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, route (e)); the active
route is the loc-triv chart-chase D1'РђЊD4' (┬Дbelow), which does NOT consume `extendScalars`/
`pullbackРѓђ`. Retained as a correct, reusable presheaf-level decomposition; do NOT extend it
toward the general build (D2 scalar-strong / D3 topological-interchange are NOT pursued). -/

section PullbackLanDecomposition

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
  {F : C РЦц D} {R : Dрхњрхќ РЦц RingCat.{u}} {S : Cрхњрхќ РЦц RingCat.{u}}

end PullbackLanDecomposition

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement Рђћ D1'РђЊD4' loc-triv pullbackРђЊtensor comparison

The locally-trivial-restricted upgrade of the oplax comparison map
`pullbackTensorMap` (`f^*(M РіЌ N) РЪХ f^*M РіЌ f^*N`) to an isomorphism, blueprint
┬Д`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, sub-lemmas D1'РђЊD4'. -/

section LocTrivPullbackTensor

/-- **The sheafified `РіЌРѓў` of the two `pullbackValIso`s (piece 4 of `pullbackTensorMap`) is an
iso.** Factored out as a top-level lemma so the `tensorIso (C := _ РІЎ forgetРѓѓ)` elaboration mirrors
the proven `tensorObjIsoOfIso` (it does not elaborate cleanly inside a tactic block carrying
`extract_lets` locals). Project-local helper for `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`. -/
private lemma isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : Y РЪХ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
      (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
        (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat))
        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))) :=
  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (­ЮЪЎ Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat))
      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f M))
      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f N)))).isIso_hom

/-- **Reduction of `pullbackTensorMap` iso-ness to the sheafified presheaf ╬┤.**

`pullbackTensorMap f M N` is the four-fold composite
`(sheafificationCompPullback).hom РЅФ a_Y.map ╬┤ РЅФ (sheafifyTensorUnitIso).hom РЅФ a_Y.map (tensorHom Рђд)`.
Three of the four factors (`sheafificationCompPullback`, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`,
and the `tensorHom` of the two `pullbackValIso`s) are isomorphisms unconditionally;
the only conditional factor is the sheafification `a_Y.map ╬┤` of the presheaf-level
oplax comparison `╬┤`. Hence `pullbackTensorMap f M N` is an iso whenever that
sheafified ╬┤ is an iso. This isolates the SOLE remaining content (the sheafified ╬┤)
for D2' (unit pair) and D4' (chart-chase). Project-local. -/
lemma isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
    (f : Y РЪХ X) (M N : X.Modules)
    (h : letI ¤є' : (X.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat) РЪХ
          (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op РІЎ (Y.presheaf РІЎ forgetРѓѓ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
          (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
        IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
          (­ЮЪЎ Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.╬┤ (PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є') M.val N.val))) :
    IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N) := by
  unfold pullbackTensorMap
  extract_lets ¤є ¤є' a_Y
  -- piece 2 (the sheafified ╬┤) is the only conditional factor Рђћ supplied by `h`.
  haveI h╬┤ : IsIso (a_Y.map
      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.╬┤ (PresheafOfModules.pullback ¤є') M.val N.val)) := h
  -- pieces 1 and 3 are `Iso.hom`s; piece 4 is `a_Y.map` of the (iso) `РіЌРѓў` of the two
  -- `pullbackValIso`s, supplied by the factored top-level helper.
  exact IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (IsIso.comp_isIso' h╬┤
    (IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso f M N)))

/-- **Converse of `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`.** A morphism of presheaves of modules whose
image under the associated-sheaf functor is an isomorphism lies (on underlying additive presheaves)
in the sheafification localizer `J.W`. This is the same morphism-property identity
`PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` (the sheafification
functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`) read backwards. Project-local:
needed to feed the flatness-free whiskering lemmas `W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_W` from a sheafified
iso (the D2' ╬и-bridge route, below). -/
private lemma W_of_isIso_sheafification {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
